title: Chamomilla suffruticosa
taxonomy: Chamomilla suffruticosa is classified in the following manner: it belongs to the family Asteraceae, is a genus within the tribe Anthemideae, and is native to the temperate regions of Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. This classification reflects its botanical and taxonomic position within the plant kingdom. The species is often grouped together with other similar plants, such as wild chamomile, due to its shared characteristics. However, more research may be needed to confirm its exact classification. It is also sometimes referred to by its synonyms, such as Anthemis suffruticosa.
uses: Chamomilla suffruticosa, also known as Roman chamomile, has been used in traditional medicine for its calming and soothing properties. Its essential oil is often used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has also been used to treat digestive issues such as indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome. Additionally, Chamomilla suffruticosa has been used to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. Its tea has been consumed to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
benefits: Chamomilla suffruticosa, also known as dwarf chamomile, has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and anxiety. Its antimicrobial properties may help to combat certain bacterial and fungal infections. Some studies suggest that it may have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it useful for treating digestive issues and muscle cramps. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy for these uses. As a herbal remedy, it is often consumed as a tea or used topically in the form of a cream or oil.
properties: Chamomilla suffruticosa is a plant that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation. The plant's essential oils, which include bisabolol and chamazulene, are thought to be responsible for its therapeutic benefits. Chamomilla suffruticosa may also have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which could help to protect against infections and promote wound healing. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic properties of this plant.
constituents: Chamomilla suffruticosa contains essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The essential oils present in this plant include chamazulene, bisabolol, and alpha-bisabolol. Flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, are also found in Chamomilla suffruticosa, contributing to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Terpenoids, including sesquiterpenes and monoterpene hydrocarbons, have been identified in the plant, which may contribute to its calming and soothing properties. These active constituents are believed to be responsible for the plant's medicinal uses in traditional medicine.
parts: Chamomilla suffruticosa is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The medicinal parts of the plant, which are primarily used for their therapeutic benefits, are the flowers and leaves. These parts are rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and other bioactive compounds that are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. The flowers are often used in herbal teas and infusions to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, while the leaves may be used in topical applications for their anti-inflammatory effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Chamomilla suffruticosa.
preparations: Chamomilla suffruticosa herbal preparations are typically made from the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots, which are rich in bioactive compounds. These preparations are often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that are valued for their potential health benefits. The plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Chamomilla suffruticosa is used to treat a range of issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects and potential uses of Chamomilla suffruticosa in herbal preparations.
side_effects: Chamomilla suffruticosa, although considered safe, may cause allergic reactions in some individuals. It may also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. Some people may experience stomach upset, dizziness, or headaches after consuming chamomilla suffruticosa. More research is needed to fully understand its potential side effects. However, chamomilla suffruticosa is generally considered safe when consumed in moderation.
intro: Chamomilla suffruticosa, also known as low-growing chamomile, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. The health benefits of Chamomilla suffruticosa include its calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, as well as sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Chamomilla suffruticosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
